decision: Create publication readiness audit and downgrade unsupported public claims.
proof: Phase 0 audit found unsupported uniqueness, outcome, publication, privacy, and reference-status issues.

decision: Revise public README wording to designed/proposed maturity and remove unsupported global uniqueness/outcome phrasing.
proof: README now uses designed-to integration and CEFR wording, potential transferability, assessment coherence, and publication exploration language.

decision: Add structured tracking, non-authoritative source-candidate matrix, lightweight checker, and hardening report.
proof: Gauntlet, YAML/JSON parse, README scans, matrix authority checks, .docx no-diff check, MC health, and git status gate all passed in the 15-iteration sweep.

decision: Record first-checker bootstrap review and strengthen revision-log schema enforcement.
proof: Checker fixtures were manually compared against the Phase 0 audit sections for required artifacts, claims, CEFR wording, privacy rules, source authority, README overclaim patterns, and revision-log fields; checker now enforces the full revision-log schema fields from the spec.

decision: Run and record a whole-build 15-iteration hardening sweep across Lingua Viva active surfaces.
proof: Clean sweep passed branch verification, gauntlet, py_compile, YAML parse, full revision-log schema, README overclaim scan, README safe anchors, audit anchors, claim classifications, matrix authority, inventory authority, .docx no-diff, active-surface MC-bloat scan, MC health, and final branch/status gates.

decision: Add a local teacher-mode support loop for doctor checks, safe repair state, incident logging, support bundle creation, privacy redaction, and improvement proposals without editing curriculum authority.
proof: Support-loop tests, py_compile, doctor, gauntlet --ci, safe repair, support-bundle creation, bundle manifest review, and Lingua Viva artifact gauntlet passed; .docx content is excluded from bundles and matrix authority remains unchanged.

decision: Build Phase A app-first Doctor MVP with local diagnostics, CLI fallback, app endpoint, and app button while deferring support bundle, repair, update, incident, and improvement workflows.
proof: Focused 15-gate hardening sweep passed: support-loop tests, app route/static tests, CLI normal and JSON output, artifact gauntlet, py_compile, YAML/NDJSON parse, endpoint smoke, no external calls, .docx no-diff, and final branch check.

decision: Supersede stale capability claims in lv-rev-006 and redact app endpoint pre-diagnostics exception detail. Phase A includes Doctor diagnostics only; support bundle, repair, update, incident logging, and improvement proposal workflows remain deferred.
proof: Review found lv-rev-006 overstated capabilities from an earlier broader draft. lv-rev-007 and this correction define the accurate MVP scope. Endpoint exception detail now returns a generic app-safe message instead of raw str(e).
